title: moderation.bsky.app / porn / one post
summary: First-party label; default-client conversion rule documented; conversion application and render effect unwitnessed.

provenance.source: labelwatch label_events stream (ATProto firehose via labelwatch ingestion)

consumer.description: Bluesky official appview + client (web / iOS / Android) running default moderation pipeline; no third-party labeler services subscribed beyond the platform defaults

render_context.policy_pipeline_version_assumed: client running @atproto/api at or near v0.19.17

documented_expected_action.action_for_post_render_under_render_cont: blur(media) behind adult-content gate placeholder; click-through required to reveal
preconditions: viewer has not opted into adult content
viewer has not overridden per-label porn setting
live client is using a policy pipeline that includes this rule

PolicyDocumentation.execution_surface_source: policy artifact behaviors block (blurs:'media', behaviors.content.contentMedia:'blur'); render-time effect, record remains hosted unchanged

LabelerEmitterDocumentation.reason: moderation.bsky.app has no app.bsky.labeler.service record ingested into labelwatch's discovery_events table (F-005 ingestion gap). Even if present, porn is already documented at the upstream_const level, so emitter declaration would be additive provenance rather than the primary citation.

PolicyWitness.reason: Public source code declares the rule, but no live receipt is observed binding 'consumer bsky.app-default-client is, at the time of this render, applying policy artifact @atproto/api@0.19.17 packages/api/src/moderation/const/labels.ts'. The default client may run a different package version; per-label settings may be overridden by the viewer; the policy pipeline may be intercepted upstream.
what_would_be_required_for_full_witness: a per-client-version reporting stream (consumer publishes its policy pipeline version)
or render-side receipts citing the policy artifact hash used at decision time

RenderObservation.reason: ATProto publishes no per-render receipts in the wire protocol. Whether any specific user, at any specific moment, saw this post blurred or hidden is invisible to Labelwatch.
what_would_be_required_for_observation: consumer-side per-render receipts in a published stream (does not currently exist in atproto)
or external probes — synthetic users loading known-labeled content and reporting observed render state. Probes give existence proofs; not population claims.

HostingObservation.reason: PolicyDocumentation.execution_surface is client_render; the hosting layer is not where this conversion acts. The record remains hosted unchanged regardless of the render-side blur.

StateBasis.derivation_source: hand-authored fixture; pinned to @atproto/api v0.19.17 HEAD 7b8c5d60a
StateBasis.note: Bundle E: fixture-author basis. freshness_horizon='unknown' because the pinned policy artifact HEAD may have moved upstream since the fixture was authored. Exporter freshness lane classifies as unknown_basis (export with caveat, never as current_basis).
